severity_definition.CRITICAL: Crash, memory corruption, undefined behavior, safety critical issue.
severity_definition.HIGH: Safety risk, hidden runtime bug, incorrect functionality.

status_definition.Partly Support: Rule is partially checked with limitations.
status_definition.Fully Support: Rule is fully implemented and validated.
status_definition.Manual Review: Rule requires manual review or human judgement.
status_definition.Not Applicable: Rule is not applicable for current project.

Name_File_001.description: File name shall only consist of alphanumeric characters and underscore. But it shall not start with underscore or numeric.

Name_File_002.description: File name shall not contain more than 31 characters.

Name_Var_001.description:
All SOFTWARE Modules shall label local variables according to the following scheme:
Composition of name: <Variable name>

Spelling of name: First letter of each word upper case, consecutive letters lower case.
The variable name should start with 'L' followed by 2 characters for data type and then the variable name.

Name_Var_002.description:
All SOFTWARE Modules shall label global variables according to the following scheme:
Composition of name: <Component name>_<Variable name>

Only one underscore between component name and variable name should be present.
Spelling of name: First letter of each word upper case, consecutive letters lower case.
The variable name should start with G followed by 2 digits for data type and then the meaningful name. Example format: <Component Name>_G<data type><variable name>.

Name_Var_003.description:
A meaningfull name (with respect to that context) should be given to the variables. Avoid giving
name as Temp, Busy, etc.

Name_Var_004.description:
Reserved identifiers, macros and functions in the standard libraries, shall not be defined, redefined
or undefined. The names of macros, objects and functions in the standard libraries shall not be reused.

Name_Func_001.description:
Global function names are structured with upper case/lower case and the first letter should be upper case.
Composition of name: <Component name>_<Function name>

Only one underscore between component name and function name.

Name_Type_001.description: Enumeration elements shall be uppercase, with words separated by the underscore character and with 'ETag’ as prefix.

Name_Type_002.description:
All SOFTWARE Modules shall label self-defined i.e. specific data types according to the following scheme:
Composition of type: <Component name>_<Type name>Type
Only one underscore between component name and type name.
Spelling of type: First letter of each word upper case, consecutive letters lower case.

Name_Macro_001.description: Constants defined as a macro shall be written in upper case. Digits and underscores are allowed but not at the start.

Style_Format_001.description: Each file shall have a file template and file description banner agreed for each project. The template should be defined at the starting of development.

Style_Format_002.description:
Items of similar purpose such as file inclusion, declaring global variables, function prototypes, etc. shall be grouped together at the top of a source file (or within a header file).

The order of these sections shall be:

For a C source file:
1. File inclusion (system includes - <xxx.h>, followed by project includes - "xxx.h")
2. Macro
3. Typedef
4. Exported global
5. Private global variables and functions

For a C header file:
1. Macro definitions
2. Typedef definitions
3. Exported global variables (extern)
4. Exported global functions

Style_Format_003.description:
All file headers shall have a revision history which includes correct information about:
1. Version.
2. Date of release/publish.
3. Change description.

Style_Format_004.description:
All function headers shall have a information about:
1. Function name.
2. Purpose.
3. Parameters.
4. Return value.

Style_Format_005.description:
Each line in source files shall be limited to 120 columns (more strict rule can be defined as project specific).
If the statement exceeds 120 characters, use a 'carriage return' and indent 2 spaces to continue the statement on the next line.

Style_Format_006.description: Tabs shall not be used instead spaces shall be used. Maintain 2 spaces difference between the levels of indentation.

Style_Format_007.description: A space shall be used between 'if', 'else if', 'while', 'for', 'switch' and the opening parenthesis.

Style_Format_008.description:
Spaces shall not be used around '.', '->' operators.
Spaces shall not be used between unary operators and operands.
Unary operators: '++', '--', '~', '!', '&' (address of), '*' (pointer).

Style_Format_009.description: A space shall be used before and after a binary operator.

Style_Format_010.description: A space shall be used after each comma in the argument or initialization list.

Style_Format_011.description: Each expression statement shall be on a separate line.

Style_Format_012.description:
Braces which enclose a block shall be placed in the same column, on separate lines directly before and after the block.

There shall not be anything else on these lines.

The block of code inside the opening and closing braces shall be indented 2 spaces.

An opening brace is always at the same level as the line above.

Exceptions:
1. The 'while' in the 'do...while' statement may be placed one space character after the brace closing the block.
2. The name of the new user type ('struct'/'union'/'enum' type) may be placed one space after the brace closing the element definitions.
3. Array initialization where the opening and closing braces are in the same line.

Style_Format_013.description: '#if' statements ('#if' - '#else' - '#endif') should be indented according to their nesting depth.

Style_Format_014.description: Tabs and unwanted spaces shall not be present at the end of the line in make files.

Style_Format_015.description: The constants shall be place on the left of equality comparisons.

Style_Format_016.description:
Check that all condition checks have variables of same type on RHS and LHS or there is explicit type casting.
RHS: right-hand side.
LHS: left-hand side.

Style_Format_017.description: Rules for the assembly language using, should be applied defined as a project-specific coding rules.

Style_File_001.description: The interface file shall have the extension '.h'and the body file shall have the extension '.c'.

Style_File_002.description: Code duplication shall be avoided. The use of functions shall be implemented to increase maintainability and portability of source code.

Style_File_003.description: The file (both header and source) should end with a blank line.

Style_File_004.description:
If a module provides several functions and processes additional module source files
“<Module>_<Sub>.c” shall be used. Meaningful Name can be chosen freely for “<Sub>”.

Style_File_005.description:
The header files should not be included back to back. One header file included in another header
file which is included back in the first header file.

Style_File_006.description: A “.c“ file shall not be included in another file: it shall be compiled and provided as an object module.

Style_Comment_001.description:
Multi-line comment blocks shall have the opening and closing character sequences in separate lines.
There shall not be anything else on these lines, but optional star frames.

Style_Comment_002.description: Comments shall comply with the ANSI-C Standard. C++ comments are not permitted.

Style_Comment_003.description:
Suitable comments shall be added for the following elements:

- Includes (for what is it needed?)
- Definition of macros
- Declaration of types
- Elements of structures and unions
- Definition of variables
- Definition of constants
- Definition of functions

Rationale:
Uniformity and readability

Style_Comment_004.description:
The following C constructs shall be commented after the concluding bracket if they are longer than 10 lines:
- switch-case
- if-then-else
- do-while
- for

Style_Comment_005.description: Constructs and compiler switches shall be commented at the end of block (e.g. after the concluding bracket, '#endif', etc.) if they are longer than 10 lines.

Style_Comment_007.description: A comment should be placed immediately above and indented same as the line/block of code which it is complementary.

Rules_Ext_001.description:
If a MISRA warning has occurred, analyse the warning to know if that error can be avoided by
modifications in the code. Or that violation of MISRA rule shall be commented and reasoned at the
corresponding code line.

Rules_Ext_002.description: Conform to the atomic data specification defined by the MCU architecture unless there is a particular request.

Rules_Envr_001.description:
The software modules have to be developed to be compliable for all processor platforms without any
changes. Any necessary processor or compiler specific instructions (e.g. memory locators, pragmas,
use of atomic bit manipulations etc.) have to be exported to macros and include files.

Rules_Envr_002.description: All SOFTWARE Modules shall not use compiler or platform specific keywords directly.

Rules_Envr_003.description:
While using object/library files (from third party, customer, etc.) it shall be confirmed that it was
produced with same compilers and version which is used in the project.

Rules_Envr_004.description: There should not be any compiler and linker warnings (of-course errors also) while building the software.

Rules_Envr_005.description:
ANSI-C does not support inline assembler and there is no general keyword for the compilers (GHS,
Renesas compiler). Therefore a macro shall be added in the compiler abstraction to simplify porting
to different compilers.

Rules_Envr_006.description: For runtime software, Compiler specific header files, libraries and intrinsic functions shall not be used.

Rules_Comment_001.description: All comments shall be in English and spell checked.

Rules_Comment_002.description:
The purpose of every line of executable code should be explained by a comment, although one
comment may describe more than one source line of code.

Rules_Comment_003.description:
Comment should be meaningful with the corresponding line of code. Avoid to stating in comments
what is better stated in code.

Rules_Comment_004.description:
Commented out code shall not be left permanently in source files. Any lines or blocks of code that
exists specifically for debugging purpose shall be surrounded by pre-processor directives for
conditional compilation (e.g. #ifdef DEBUG … #endif)

Rules_Comment_005.description: File function header format should be consistent across the code.

Rules_Comment_006.description:
Each cast shall feature an associated comment describing how the code ensures proper behaviour
across the range of possible values on the right side.

Rules_Identifier_001.description: Identifiers shall not have both internal and external linkage in the same translation unit.

Rules_Types_001.description: Enumeration types shall be used instead of integer constants for limited choices.

Rules_Types_002.description: Integer values of enumeration elements shall not be used in calculations.

Rules_Types_003.description: Identifiers shall not contain the ‘_’ character twice in succession.

Rules_Types_004.description: Loop counter variables shall be declared using generic types (e.g. ‘uint’).

Rules_Const_001.description: Hexadecimal constants shall be represented using all uppercase letters following 0x.

Rules_Const_002.description:
Numeric values shall not be used in code; symbolic values shall be used instead.

Exception:
1. 0 in initialization of variables.
2. 1 in bit operations.
3. Constant.

Rules_Const_003.description: All SOFTWARE Modules shall indicate all global data with read-only purposes by explicitly assigning the 'const' keyword.

Rules_Defn_Decl_001.description: Global variables must be avoided or its usage needs to be justified.

Rules_Defn_Decl_002.description: The 'register' storage class specifier must not be used.

Rules_Defn_Decl_003.description:
A structure or enumeration type variable shall not be defined in the type definition itself. It means
each self-defined type has to have an explicit type declaration even if there is only one variable of
this type.

Rules_Defn_Decl_004.description: Multiple variable declarations shall not be allowed on the same line.

Rules_Defn_Decl_005.description:
A global/exported object or function of a module shall be declared with ‘extern’ only in that
particular module header file.

Rules_Defn_Decl_006.description: Each implementation file shall have the corresponding header file and an optional private header file.

Rules_Defn_Decl_007.description: A private header file must not be used by implementation files other than the one it corresponds to.

Rules_Defn_Decl_008.description:
Variables and functions (other than macros) shall not be defined within in an ‘.h’ file. They shall be
defined within the module’s C file.

Rules_Defn_Decl_009.description:
Directly use compiler and platform specific inline keywords like ‘__inline__’ or ‘_inline’ are not
allowed. To define an inline function, macro (e.g. LOCAL_INLINE) shall be used.

Rules_Defn_Decl_010.description:
Declarations of functions shall always be stated with detailed parameter list, i.e. the type and a
practical designation of the relevant parameters.
Designator names in ‘.c’ and ‘.h’ file shall be identical.

Rules_Defn_Decl_011.description:
Declaration and definition of local functions shall have the storage-class specifier ‘static’.
Local function means function with internal linkage (only visible inside the module).

Rules_Defn_Decl_012.description: Variables used in the code need to be defined; moreover, unused variables should not be contained.

Rules_Defn_Decl_013.description: Unused input parameter(s) of function shall not be declared.

Rules_Defn_Decl_014.description:
The length of the register in the device header file and the CPU manual should be verified and its
implementation and the value generation details should be verified across variants to avoid illegal
access of that register.

Rules_Defn_Decl_015.description:
The ‘volatile’ keyword should be used to indicate variables that may be modified outside of the
program’s control (i.e., any register values that are set or cleared by hardware, or memory that is
dual-port and may be modified by an external device).

Rules_Defn_Decl_016.description:
The common implementations across the modules such as initialization of status of the driver should
be consistent across all the modules.

Rules_Defn_Decl_017.description:
The variables and macros (used in different modules but similar context), shall be named similarly.
Consistency should be maintained across modules.

Rules_Defn_Decl_018.description: The type of variable should be appropriately selected depending on the values that the variable may hold.

Rules_Defn_Decl_019.description:
Typecasts and the values assigned to variables, the compiler abstractions to it shall be correctly
defined and shall be consistent across the modules

Rules_Defn_Decl_020.description: No multiple uses of variable names.

Rules_Defn_Decl_021.description: Bit-fields shall not be defined within signed integer types.

Rules_Defn_Decl_022.description: If a function parameter is not going to be changed inside the function then it should be made a constant parameter.

Rules_Defn_Decl_023.description: The keyword 'auto' shall not be used ('auto' is the default storage class for local variables).

Rules_Defn_Decl_024.description:
Variables shall be defined in the appropriate scope. for example, the variable that is defined in the
same file and is accessed from multiple functions, this variables declared as static at file scope.

Rules_Defn_Decl_025.description: The variables should have only one purpose. should not have multiple purpose.

Rules_Defn_Decl_026.description:
The Type and size of the variables are suited to its intended use.
e.g. Do not increase the occupied size by taking on extra the size of the variable.

Rules_Init_001.description: All variables and pointers shall be initialized to its default value before its usage. The declaration and initialization shall be in different lines.

Rules_Expr_001.description:
Brackets shall always be used in complex expressions even if the C priority rules do not necessarily
demand this for operators. . This also applies to expressions evaluated by the pre-processor.

Rules_Expr_002.description:
Control structure nesting should be minimized and not exceed 4 levels. The number of levels shall
be customizable. That means that it shall be possible to reduce the allowed number of levels per file.

Rules_Expr_003.description: Multiple assignments shall not be done.

Rules_Expr_004.description:
The use of '++' and '--' should be limited to simple cases.  They shall not be used in statements where
other operators occur. The prefix use is always forbidden.

Rules_Expr_005.description: While performing masking operation, make sure of the mask value to be used and its impact for other functionalities.

Rules_Expr_006.description: Check for wrong usage of '&&', '||' while performing mask operations in the CODE.

Rules_Expr_007.description: If the division operation is being performed in the embedded code then make sure to avoid condition of illegal division by zero.

Rules_Expr_008.description:
During updating for similar part of code (e.g. timer unit/channel interrupt handlers), check the
code (e.g. unit/channel numbers) carefully, because there can be typo mistakes in updating
correct values of those numbers.

Rules_Expr_009.description: Try to avoid comparison of RAM variable in the loops to reduce the CPU load.

Rules_Expr_010.description: Considering integer boundaries (overflows & underflows) while handling arithmetic operation.

Rules_Expr_011.description: No dynamic objects or variables, or else online test during their creation.

Rules_Expr_012.description: Type conversions of the variables shall be done explicitly and correctly. It shall not be implicit.

Rules_Expr_013.description: None of the bit-wise operators (i.e. '&', '|', '~', '^', '<<', '>>') shall be used to manipulate signed integer data.

Rules_CtrlFlow_001.description: The controlling expressions shall not contain function call.

Rules_CtrlFlow_002.description: Assignments shall not be made within an 'if' or 'else if' or loop controlling expression.

Rules_CtrlFlow_003.description:
Cyclomatic complexity numbers of all functions shall be minimized and be limited for each project
(basically <= 10 for each ASIL level is reasonable). They shall be analysed and documented. The
limitation should be set less than 30 dependent on the specific project.

Rules_CtrlFlow_004.description: 'break' and 'continue' commands should never be used in loops except under very rare circumstances when there is no other option.

Rules_CtrlFlow_005.description: Any 'if' statement shall end with an 'else' clause.

Rules_CtrlFlow_006.description:
Avoid endless loop conditions in the source code (especially the loops in source code which
have exit conditions depending on changes in the status of registers). Any loop should also
have a condition to exit at some points of time by using some count values which are
appropriate for the functionality of that loop.

Rules_CtrlFlow_007.description:
The ‘break’ should be between each ‘case’ statement, unless the ‘case’ statements need to be
executed in sequence in switch statements (mentioned exception is allowed with regard to faster
processing speed, higher code efficiency, etc.).

Rules_CtrlFlow_008.description: The write protected registers should be identified. The special sequence of using protection command registers and status registers to update those protected registers should be followed.

Rules_CtrlFlow_009.description:
No hidden data flow or control flow except for call back function defined by SOFTWARE. “No hidden
data flow or control flow” are meant to prohibit a control flow from processing multiple meanings.
This is the rule to control the increase in risk of occurrence of problems by implementing a control
which is not recognized by a designer.

Rules_CtrlFlow_010.description: No unconditional jumps. Unconditional jumps like ‘goto’ shall not be used.

Rules_CtrlFlow_011.description:
When you use 'switch', 'while', 'do-while', 'for' statement, even if you can write one sentence,
writing enclosed within the brace { }.

Rules_CtrlFlow_012.description: When use “if-else” or “switch”. All conditions should be handled.

Rules_CtrlFlow_013.description:
There shall be no unreachable code.
e.g. delete the branch code by impossible conditions.

Rules_Func_001.description: Functions that do not return a parameter shall be declared as 'void' type.

Rules_Func_002.description: A function shall not return a pointer to a local object.

Rules_Func_003.description: If a function returns a value, the calling code should check for an absence of errors.

Rules_Func_004.description:
One entry and one exit point in subprograms and functions. The way to invoke one function should
be same and consistent. The return for exit should be one in a subprogram and a function.

Rules_Func_005.description: No recursions. Recursive functions shall not be used.

Rules_Func_006.description:
All services and APIs of a module shall be multicore re-entrant.

Re-entrant definition (single core): A function is re-entrant if it can be interrupted somewhere in
its execution and then safely called again before its previous invocation completes executing,
produce correct results for both invocations.

Re-entrant definition (multiple cores): A function is re-entrant if it can be called on one core while
it is already executing on any other core and still produce correct results for all calls.

Rules_Func_007.description: All scheduled functions of a module shall be multicore re-entrant.

Rules_Ptr_001.description: Limited use of pointers. Avoid abuse of pointers for unnecessary cases and consider design to avoid usage of pointers.

Rules_Ptr_002.description: A structure as parameter for a function should be passed as pointer.

Rules_Ptr_003.description: Pointers shall be checked before being de-referenced.

Rules_Ptr_004.description: A 'typedef' shall be used to simplify program syntax when declaring function pointers.

Rules_Ptr_005.description: To address a structure's fields via a pointer to the structure, the notation 'Ptr->Field' shall be used.

Rules_Struct_001.description: All members of a structure shall be named and shall only be accessed via their names.

Rules_PreProcess_001.description:
Header files which are part of predefined program libraries shall be included using '< >'.
Header files which are part of the source code generated in the software project shall be included
with ‘””’ (double quotes symbols).

Rules_PreProcess_002.description: Each header file shall protect itself against multiple inclusions.

Rules_PreProcess_003.description: Each module shall include its own header file.

Rules_PreProcess_004.description:
A file should include all the file they need and not rely on secondary includes (notice that only files
which are really required shall be included).

Rules_PreProcess_005.description: Path shall not be specified (absolute or relative) for the declaration of the included file.

Rules_PreProcess_006.description: Direct values (fixed value, addresses, etc.) should not be hard-coded in the source code. Macro ('#define', 'constant', etc.) should be used preferably.

Rules_PreProcess_007.description: Only the following pre-processor directives shall be used: '#if', '#elif', '#else', '#endif', '#ifdef', '#ifndef', '#define', '#include', '#error' and '#pragma'.

Rules_PreProcess_008.description:
Compiler switches shall be compared with defined values. Simple checks if a compiler switch is
defined shall not be used. In general ‘ON’ and ‘OFF’ shall be used to switch functionality on or off.
These symbols and their values are defined in a header file which define common standard types.

Rules_PreProcess_009.description: Macros used in place of functions should have the same notation as a function.

Rules_PreProcess_010.description:
If a macro definition represents several statements, the entire statement shall be encapsulated
within curly brackets. For multi-lines macro definitions use the ‘\’ (backslash or yen) symbol at the
end of line.

Rules_PreProcess_011.description:
In order to avoid hidden unintended type conversions, the explicit casting in macros for macro
parameters shall not be allowed. If type casting is needed, it shall be done explicitly by the macro
caller. Exceptions shall be documented.

Rules_PreProcess_012.description: Macros shall not use global names, since the global names may be hidden by a local declaration.

Rules_Opt_002.description: Combine equivalent constants, however, this is at the cost of readability. Appropriate comments should be provided where necessary.

Rules_Opt_003.description: The condition of most probable occurrence should be placed in the 'if' rather than in the 'else'.

Rules_Opt_004.description:
Substitute operations to conserve resources. Optimize '|' (or) and '&' (and) expressions. An
‘|’/’&’ query should be held as short as possible:
- At an ‘|’ query, put the most probable case in the first statement.
- At an ‘&’ query, put the most improbable case in the first statement.
- For the query of the same variables use a mask.

Rules_Opt_005.description: The time for the execution of interrupt service routine should be minimal. The delay in case of nested interrupts is to be documented.

Rules_Opt_006.description: Pre-processor macros/configuration data/source code shall be memory mapped to the correct section.

Rules_Opt_007.description:
Memory mapping shall not be used for variables defined within a function. Memory mapping header
files shall not be included inside the body of a function.

Rules_Opt_008.description:
Avoid wastage of bytes by aligning variables in memory:
- 16-bit variables have to be located at an address divisible by 2.
- 32-bit variables have to be located at an address divisible by 4.
It is recommended to sort by size (e.g. first all pointers, then all 32-bit variables, then all 16-bit
variables and then all 8-bit variables).
